name: mangomango

metadata.name: mangomango
metadata.summary: A simple MongoDB client that works with Pydantic models.

documentation:
# MangoMango


## Basics

### Usage

#### Connecting to MongoDB

To connect to a MongoDB instance, create a `MangoMango` client:

```python
from mangomango import MangoMango

client = MangoMango(host="localhost", port=27017)
```

#### Creating a Database

To create or get a database, use the `get_database` method:

```python
db = client.get_database("mydatabase")
```

#### Defining a Table

To define a table, use the `table` decorator on a Pydantic model. The type will not change, it will just register a table with this Model.
To get the table, use get_table:

```python
@db.table(collection="users", primary_key="id")
class User(BaseModel):
    id: int
    username: str
    email: str

UserTable = db.get_table("users")
```

#### Inserting Data

To insert data into a table:

```python
user = User(id=0, username="john_doe", email="john@example.com")
UserTable.insert(user)
```

#### Querying Data

To query data from a table:

```python
user = UserTable.find({"username": "john_doe"})
print(user.username, user.email)
```

#### Updating Data

To update data in a table, use the update() method. The first argument is the primary key of the object, it can also be the object itself.

```python
UserTable.update(john.id, {"$set": {"email": "john_new@example.com"}})
```

You can also edit the object and re-push it with the same primary key. use push() method that insert if there is no object with the same primary key, else it remove and then insert

```python
john.email = "john_new@example.com"
UserTable.push(john)
```

#### Deleting Data

To delete one data from a table, use remove() method.
Can take either primary key or object as first argument:

```python
UserTable.remove(john.id)
```

#### Bulk Insert

To insert multiple objects:

```python
users = [
    User(id=1, username="alice", email="alice@example.com"),
    User(id=2, username="bob", email="bob@example.com")
]
UserTable.insert_many_iter(users)
```

#### Bulk Update

To update multiple objects:

```python
UserTable.update_many({"username": {"$in": ["alice", "bob"]}}, {"$set": {"active": True}})
```

#### Bulk Delete

To delete multiple objects:

```python
UserTable.remove_many({"username": {"$in": ["alice", "bob"]}})
```

classes.name: mangomango.MangoMangoError
documentation: Base class for all exceptions raised by MangoMango.

classes.name: mangomango.ObjectAlreadyExists
documentation: Raised when trying to insert an object that already exists in the table.

classes.name: mangomango.TableNotFound
documentation: Raised when trying to get a table that is not registered in the database object.

documentation: Create a MangoMango client.

methods.name: mangomango.MangoMango.get_database
documentation: Get a database from the client.

class_variables.name: mangomango.MangoMango.client
documentation: The pymongo.MongoClient instance used by the client.

class_variables.name: mangomango.MangoMango.cached_databases
documentation: The databases stuctures that have been cached by the client. Should not be used directly.
A MongoDB client wich stores the databases with their typed tables.

classes.name: mangomango.Database

methods.name: mangomango.Database.__init__
documentation: Create a database object. Should not be used directly.

documentation: Get a table from the database by its name.

documentation: Add a table to the database.

methods.name: mangomango.Database.table
documentation: Decorator to add a table to the database.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Database.client
documentation: The MangoMango client that is used to connect to the database.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Database.name
documentation: The name of the database.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Database.tables
documentation: The tables that have been registred in the code (not all the existing tables).
Represent a database that contains the tables.

classes.name: mangomango.Table

methods.name: mangomango.Table.__init__
documentation: Create a table object.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.load_object
documentation: Load a document from the database (or not) into the table model.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.get_id_of
documentation: Get the primary key of the given object.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.find
documentation: Find objects in the table that match the query.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.find_one
documentation: Find one object in the table that match the query.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.get
documentation: Get an object from the table by its primary key.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.insert
documentation: Insert an object in the table.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.insert_many_iter
documentation: Insert multiple objects in the table. (Iterative version)

methods.name: mangomango.Table.update_many
documentation: Update objects in the table that are mathing the filter.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.remove_many
documentation: Remove objects from the table that are mathing the filter.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.update
documentation: Update an object in the table by its primary key.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.remove
documentation: Remove an object from the table by its primary key.

methods.name: mangomango.Table.push
documentation: Insert or update an object in the table depending if it already exists.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Table.model
documentation: The Pydantic model which validate the objects in the table.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Table.collection
documentation: The name of the MongoDB database collection.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Table.database
documentation: The database that the table is rattached to.

class_variables.name: mangomango.Table.primary_key
documentation: The primary key of all the objects in the table that is used to identify them.
A table in a database with an associated model.
